title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to spend the entire day going through drive-throughs. Is it good for my diet? Absolutely not. But I hope by the end of watching this video, you are much more comfortable and confident going through a drive-through in the US. So wish my stomach luck.
Come on, I'm going to be zero left. No, I will not be. Okay, I'll do a sausage egg McMuffin meal with orange juice, please.
No, but Monday, Friday, the weekday is going to be 10, 30. It stops and the weekend is 11.
Pro tip. Always ask for sauces. You can't assume they're going to put them in your bag.
Haven't been here for a hot minute though, so I'm going to need a minute to look over the menu.

title: will / shall
examples: Come on, I'm going to be zero left. No, I will not be. Okay, I'll do a sausage egg McMuffin meal with orange juice, please.
Sometimes I don't catch what they say and I just ask them to repeat themselves when that happens. Usually I'll say, I'm sorry, I didn't catch that. Or I'm sorry, what was that?
Impeccable service. Fast with a smile, well done McDonald's. I hope they threw some ketchup in the bag.
And I'll do an order of the egg white bites.
And I will also do an iced matcha latte.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: And sausage egg and cheese McGrittle meal also with orange juice, please. And can I just ask how long do you serve breakfast?
I just ordered through, drive-through. Can I have some ketchup to go?
Pro tip. Always ask for sauces. You can't assume they're going to put them in your bag.
Could I please get a minute?
What can I get for you today?

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: I must have eaten at least 5,000 calories today.

title: should (advice)
examples: No, but I think that should be it.
Maybe I should have kept the classic syrup.
That should tell you how broke we were.
No one should eat this much fast food in a day.
Yep, and I think that should be it.

title: should have (done)
examples: Maybe I should have kept the classic syrup.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: Would you like to keep the classic or no?
I thought that at a franchise like Starbucks, it would be the same.
After a night at the club, we would hit up Taco Bell.
But if we were feeling like splurging a little bit, we would go to Taco Bell.
Would you like to try your orange chicken today?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you get McDonald's breakfast, this is the only order you need to know.
But if we were feeling like splurging a little bit, we would go to Taco Bell.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Okay, wow. So we made it just in time, huh? Yeah. Okay, perfect. I think that'll be it for us today. We almost didn't make it in time.
Ordering at Starbucks is not for the week.
And I think that'll be it for today.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: No, but I think that should be it.
Yep, and I think that should be it.

title: have something done
examples: I almost want to get a orange just to see how big it is.
Have a good night.
